progression.notes: Per-visit machinery, deliberately ONE stage: the byte's values are the join/leave conversation and walk nudges, EVENT_IN_SAFARI_ZONE flips per visit, and the step/ball counters are the global Safari bytes (0x29B9/0x2CF3, re-seeded at the gate each game). Nothing here is a story progression.

states.name: The gate, between games
states.desc: The reception desk in its resting shape. Everything this map writes (the in-Safari flag, the walk nudges, the counters) is per-visit machinery re-armed each time -- there is no later stage to roll to.

notes: Safari steps-left (0x29B9, big-endian) and balls (0x2CF3) live on the Map Storage panel's Safari Zone page.

states.name: Cutscene step: SCRIPT_SAFARIZONEGATE_PLAYER_MOVING_RIGHT
states.desc: Mid-cutscene machinery between stages; a save loaded here resumes inside the cutscene.
